version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release — exports the project's markers and regions as a printable PDF setlist. Song detection combines four strategies (Song-Lane by colour, Regions=Songs, prefix trigger, blacklist veto). Time base H:MM:SS or bar.beat. Songs rendered bold with running number; length shown for songs only (region: end-start; marker: until next song). Footer with song count, net-music, gross duration. PDF saved next to .rpp and opened in the OS reader; no external dependencies (Helvetica/Helvetica-Bold built in). Optional "Save As…" (requires js_ReaScriptAPI) and "Copy as Text" (requires SWS).

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: GUI: labels now appear BEFORE their input fields (prefix trigger, blacklist) so it's clear which control is which. Lanes: try to parse Reaper-7 named ruler lanes from the project chunk and group by lane name when available — falls back to (type + colour) grouping when no named lanes are detected. Adds a "Debug: dump chunk" button (SWS) that copies the project's marker/lane chunk lines to the clipboard for diagnosis of the lane format.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix crash on startup when SWS is not installed: GetProjectStateChunk does not exist in stock Reaper. Falls back to reading the .rpp file directly (works for any saved project). Debug dump now also works without SWS — writes a "chunk-dump.txt" next to the .rpp instead of using the clipboard.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix blacklist over-matching: blacklist words now match only when the marker name STARTS with that word followed by a word boundary, instead of substring-matching anywhere. Previously a generic "Intro" entry would falsely veto song markers like "Élida Almeida: Intro zu Txika"; now only categorical names like "Intro", "Intro 1", "Beifall …", "Ansage Maude" get vetoed. GUI label updated to reflect the new behaviour.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Lane name detection now actually works: parse the project chunk's RULERLANE entries with the real format Reaper 7+ uses ("RULERLANE idx flag name color …" — name is unquoted for single-word lanes, quoted otherwise). Reaper-7-named lanes ("Songs", "Marker", "Region") now appear by name in the GUI's lane table; the color/type fallback only kicks in when no named lanes exist. Marker-lane lookup keyed by (type, idx) so markers and regions sharing index numbers don't collide.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Live preview + per-row override: a scrollable preview pane below the lane table shows exactly what the PDF will contain. The "Song" checkbox per row overrides the auto-classification for that single marker (persists by marker GUID, survives renaming and moving). An asterisk after the name marks an active override; flipping the override back to the auto value removes it. Marker colour swatch in the PDF: a small coloured square left of the row number reflects each marker's Reaper colour (skipped for default-coloured markers). PDF column layout shifted 10pt right to make room.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix crash in preview pane: ImGui_BeginChild's 5th argument is now child_flags (a number) instead of the old border boolean. Use ImGui_ChildFlags_Border() if available, else 0.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Remove marker colour swatch from PDF and preview pane (visual noise outweighed the benefit). PDF column layout reverted to the pre-1.1.0 positions. Preview pane drops the swatch column. PDF content stream simplified back to text-only.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

reapack.name: JG_Convert_Items_To_Tempo_Map.lua
reapack.desc: JG Convert Items To Tempo Map

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Moved from Pro to free Tools – creates named tracks from clipboard text, detects stereo pairs, optional 1:1 HW routing for Virtual Soundcheck

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release — moves cursor to next item edge on selected tracks (or all tracks if none selected). Back-to-back edges count as one position and select the following item; gaps select the item just ended. View stays centered on cursor.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Treat crossfades as a single transition: overlapping items on the same track now collapse to one edge at the next item's start, just like a back-to-back cut. Cursor lands on the start edge, the following item is selected.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release — moves cursor to previous item edge on selected tracks (or all tracks if none selected). Mirrored logic of "Next": back-to-back edges select the preceding item; gaps select the item just started. View stays centered on cursor.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Always prefer the item that starts at the edge position (consistent with "Next"). Only fall back to the ending item when no item starts at the edge (pure end edge with gap following).

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Treat crossfades as a single transition: overlapping items on the same track now collapse to one edge at the next item's start, just like a back-to-back cut. Cursor lands on the start edge, the following item is selected.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release — one empty, beat-locked item per measure on a pinned, height-locked track, labelled with the printed score measure number. Anchors via markers named "=NN" restart the numbering; without anchors timeline measure 1 = printed measure 1.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Stretched label text (IMGRESOURCEFLAGS), doubled track height (60px), English track name "Score Measures", black track/item colour (white text in the default theme; switch LABEL_COLOR to white if your theme draws dark text).

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Tint =NN anchor markers (default red) on each run so renumbering / repeat points are visible at a glance. Configurable via COLOR_ANCHORS / ANCHOR_COLOR.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Respect the project "start measure offset" setting: default (un-anchored) numbering now follows REAPER's displayed bar numbers via format_timestr_pos. Default label colour changed to white so the dark default item text stays readable.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release — rides destination track(s) to follow a reference's macro dynamics. Measures the source reference short-term loudness (BS.1770-4 K-weighting) and writes a smoothed Pre-FX volume envelope onto the destinations (louder where the reference is loud, quieter where quiet). ReaImGui GUI: capture source/destination tracks, Follow amount (depth), Inertia, Max boost/cut. Time-selection aware, silence follows to Max cut. Requires ReaImGui.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Replace the separate Max boost / Max cut sliders with a single intuitive "Ride range (dB)" two-handle range (lower/upper limit, right = more level). Add an inline explanation of Follow amount (slope) vs the range limits. Old maxboost/maxcut settings migrate automatically.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Selectable source tap point (Item raw / Track post-FX / Post-fader incl. source volume automation) and destination apply point (Pre-FX volume envelope or a self-contained gain JSFX at the end of the chain, leaving the volume envelope free). Item-raw temporarily bypasses source-track FX; post-fader folds in the source volume envelope. Both choices via radio buttons.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix Gain JSFX destination mode: write the dB value directly to the FX parameter envelope (a JSFX slider's value space is its own range, not normalised 0..1), so the ride scales over the full Ride range instead of tiny 0..1 dB moves.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Gain JSFX mode now reuses the existing JG Ride Gain instance by scanning the FX chain by name (any position) instead of relying on AddByName query semantics — re-syncing updates the same plugin instead of risking a duplicate, and you can move it anywhere in the chain (e.g. before a reverb) and it keeps working.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Status line now reports per-destination failures explicitly (e.g. "Could not create the gain-JSFX envelope on: <track>") so an empty result is diagnosable instead of silent.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Status line now shows the analysed time range and flags "[LIMITED to time selection!]" when an active time selection is shortening the analysis — makes a too-short ride (e.g. only the first second written) immediately obvious.

reapack.name: JG_Rename_Source_Files_To_Item_Names.lua
reapack.desc: JG Rename Source Files To Item Names

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

reapack.desc: JG Sort Items To Tracks By Prefix

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

reapack.name: JG_Transfer_Item_Cuts_Gaps_And_Fades.lua
reapack.desc: JG Transfer Item Cuts Gaps And Fades

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Embed JSON library directly (fix ReaPack install)

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix ImGui_PushFont missing font size argument

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix color picker (was using wrong format), auto-coloring, English UI, case-insensitive aliases

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Add case rules (ALL UPPERCASE / all lowercase) with highest priority, remove skip folders option

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Replace case rules with uppercase darkening: UPPERCASE tracks get their resolved color darkened by configurable %. Children inherit the undarkened color.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Major restructure: remove top-level rules (modules only), remove parent filter, remove tabs (flat UI), comma-separated rule pattern aliases, per-module folder darken slider (replaces fixed folder color), tracks without folder context match all modules in order

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Module color as central color source, per-rule "use module color" toggle (default on), remove uppercase darkening, folder aliases as comma-separated string, module folder gets darkened module color (default 10%), migration from v2.0 data

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Module name = first alias (no separate name field), stale P_EXT cleanup on each engine run, random colors for unmatched tracks (optional), folder darken default 0, orphaned file cleanup on save

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Show 0xRRGGBB hex code next to color pickers

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Drag & drop reordering for rules and modules, visible color picker with hex input, Sort A-Z for rules, new rules default to module color, reset track color when moved out of folder

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Auto specificity sorting: engine matches exact > starts_with/ends_with > contains, longer patterns first. GUI shows rules alphabetically. Manual rule reordering removed.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix child inheritance: user-colored tracks now reliably pass their color to children. New rules stay at bottom, stable ImGui IDs for empty rules.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix black track bug: use I_CUSTOMCOLOR to properly reset to theme default. Remove random_unmatched option.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix crash when collapsing window (ImGui_End only when Begin returns true)

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Background mode: script runs continuously for auto-coloring without GUI. Run action again to toggle GUI. Close window hides GUI but keeps engine running. Works with SWS startup actions.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix invalid ImGui context when toggling GUI back on after background mode. Add Stop button. Lazy context creation.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Performance: replace fingerprint polling with GetProjectStateChangeCount for instant change detection. Cache alias lookup. Localize REAPER API calls in engine loop. Module delete button next to + with confirmation dialog.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Per-module opt-in "Apply rules to tracks without module context". Modules can now decide individually whether their rules should color standalone tracks (tracks not under a folder matching any module alias). Tracks with a module context are still handled only by that module. When multiple standalone-enabled modules match, the most specific rule wins (tie-break: module order).

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Per-rule "S" (Standalone) and "G" (Global) flags replace the module-level Standalone toggle. S (default ON) controls whether a rule colors tracks without module context. G (default OFF) makes a rule override foreign module contexts: e.g. a "Reverb" rule in the FX module marked Global will color a Reverb track inside Drums with the FX color, while still respecting FX's own rules when the track sits in FX. Existing module-level Standalone settings are migrated onto each of that module's rules.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Fix Global rule precedence: global rules from foreign modules now correctly win over the surrounding module's rules. Previously a track like "REV Bongo Snare Ride" inside Drums would match the Drums "Bongo"/"Snare"/"Ride" rules before falling through to the global REV rule. Own-module rules are still untouched (global rules from the home module are excluded from the override pass).

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: No more undo points: remove Undo_BeginBlock/EndBlock around the engine. Auto coloring no longer floods the undo history with "Track Auto Color" entries on every item edit, fade or selection change.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Performance: 2-stage gate + 100 ms throttle. Engine now only runs when track structure actually changes (count, names, folder depth, colors). Item trim, fades, selection changes etc. no longer trigger a full color pass.

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Tracks without a module folder context are no longer colored (no cross-module fallback). Folder aliases now support match modes: contains (default), starts_with, ends_with, exact.

reapack.name: JG_Track_Auto_Color_Toggle_GUI.lua
reapack.desc: JG Track Auto Color - Toggle GUI

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release — toggles the GUI of the background Track Auto Color engine

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release

version.author: Julius Gass
version.changelog: Initial release
